text:
[2026-04-14 12:48] Welcome back. Acknowledged your three parked items — T06 Phase 3 amendment, T09 v3 loop-in, polyphony-dev amendments — all stay parked. Session scope is the audit below.

**Task #2 moved to `in_progress`.**

## Session context you need

This session started with a janitorial discovery: 36 files (~175KB) of per-user auto-memory were sitting **untracked** at `.claude/teams/framework-research/project-memory/` in the shared `mitselek/ai-teams` repo, plus a `.project-dir-name` marker with my machine-specific slug. Source: your WIP `persist-project-state.sh` ran against this workstation's repo and mirrored `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/*.md` into `project-memory/`. Team-lead deleted both before any commit.

Cal filed the gotcha: `wiki/gotchas/persist-project-state-leaks-per-user-memory.md`, committed as **`f8cb4e8`**. He framed it as "same code, two substrates, opposite invariants" — mirror semantics are correct for container-scoped team repos, wrong for multi-workstation shared team repos. He enumerated three mitigation options but explicitly refused to pick one: **that decision is yours.**

## Your read list (in order)

1. `wiki/gotchas/persist-project-state-leaks-per-user-memory.md` — Cal's gotcha (blocking prerequisite, as you already noted)
2. `wiki/gotchas/dual-team-dir-ambiguity.md` — Cal added a "Wrong-Substrate Variant" section pointing at his new entry; sibling gotcha
3. `docs/persist-coverage-design-v0.1.md`, `v0.2.md`, `v0.3.md` — your own design evolution, the ship target is v0.3
4. `docs/protocol-a-submission-draft.md` — I don't know what this is yet; open question in the audit
5. The four scripts: `persist-project-state.sh`, `restore-project-state.sh`, `persist-session-logs.sh`, `restore-session-logs.sh`

## What I want from you

A **readiness assessment** — this is a review session, not a ship session. Framework-research is on-hold while focus sits on uikit-dev; the actual ship decision for these scripts belongs to a future focused session. You are producing a report, not a commit.

Report structure:

1. **Per-script verdict** — for each of the four scripts, one of: `ship as-is`, `ship with Nx change` (list the changes), or `rework` (say why). Check against v0.3 spec: is it aligned, or has the script drifted from the design, or has the design drifted from what the script needs?

2. **Chosen mitigation** from Cal's three options:
   - (a) container-only runtime guard — refuse to run unless a sentinel indicates container substrate
   - (b) `.gitignore` entry — cover `project-memory/` + `.project-dir-name`
   - (c) target-dir refusal — resolve the target, check against a shared-repo sentinel, abort if match
   
   Pick one (or combine two as layered defense, if justified). Explain the trade-off you weighed. **Do not implement it this session** — the pick lands in your report and waits for the ship session.

3. **`protocol-a-submission-draft.md` decision** — what is it, who's the intended recipient, should it go out, and if so, through which channel. If it's stale/superseded, say so and I'll have Cal process the deletion separately.

4. **Any structural-discipline flags** — if the audit surfaces a post-bootstrap gap (prompt references something that doesn't exist, path that resolves to two roots, typed-contract field drift between producer and consumer), flag it per the 4-gate rule in common-prompt.md. Separate section.

## Hard constraints

- **Read-only on the scripts.** Don't edit, don't stage, don't `chmod +x`, don't run them (dry-run or otherwise). The v0.3 design has a `--dry-run` mode precisely because live testing is sensitive — this session isn't the field test.
- **No wiki writes.** Any gotcha/pattern/observation that surfaces during your audit goes through Cal via Protocol A after this session, not during it.
- **No script commits.** Even a `.gitignore` patch waits for the ship session.
- **Stay inside the 4 gates when you read across artifacts.** Gate 2 (producer↔consumer cross-read) especially applies — if v0.3 says one thing and a script does another, that's the kind of silent drift I want surfaced.

When done: `TaskUpdate #2 → completed`, `SendMessage` me your report (structured per the four sections above). No shell artifacts, no committed changes — just the assessment.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: Brief Volta on persist script audit

text:
[2026-04-14 12:54] Audit accepted. Quality work. Specific calls worth flagging back to you:

**Mitigation pick is affirmed.** (c) + `git check-ignore` opt-in is the right answer. The escape-hatch semantic — "container teams opt in via team-local `.gitignore` to assert intent; workstations without that assertion get hard refusal" — correctly inverts (b)'s fail-open into a fail-closed with explicit opt-in. Your rejection of (a) on the "unenforceable cross-team invariant" grounds is the right lens: you caught yourself repeating the v0.1 rejection pattern and applied it consistently. That's the discipline working.

One edge case I want you to document in the helper comments (not during this session — just flag it in the saved report so the ship session picks it up): **repo-root `.gitignore` footgun.** A future operator who doesn't understand the semantic could add `project-memory/` to the TOP-LEVEL repo `.gitignore` to "silence the warning," which would satisfy `git check-ignore` and open the leak back up. It's a thin risk (requires affirmative action and some context), but your helper's comment block should call out that the opt-in is intended to be **team-local** (`.claude/teams/<team>/.gitignore` or similar narrow-scope), not repo-root. A helper comment + a defensive test-the-check-ignore-path-doesn't-contain-git-toplevel wouldn't hurt either. Flag for ship session, don't implement now.

**Flag 3 ordering call is load-bearing** — mitigation before marker re-creation, not after. I've captured that explicitly in my scratchpad as a ship-session invariant. Order violation there re-enables the exact defect Cal filed.

**Flag 1 is substrate-invariant-mismatch n=3 territory.** The v0.3 skill patches using `$TEAM_DIR` ambiguously is a NEW consumer of the same root cause as `dual-team-dir-ambiguity.md`. Cal has a pattern candidate queued for the next non-freeze session (n=2 + your supporting data point = stronger promotion case). I've noted your Flag 1 in my scratchpad as supporting evidence. You do NOT need to submit this to Cal — I'll relay on the pattern drafting session.

**`protocol-a-submission-draft.md` disposition accepted.** HOLD + STATUS UPDATE + post-mitigation revision pass. Direct routing to Cal per dual-hub rule is correct. Keep the file, don't touch it this session.

---

## Task for you before shutdown

The report you just sent is too dense for the session transcript to be the only durable copy. Save it as a doc file in the repo so the ship session can grep it and reference it.

1. **Write** `.claude/teams/framework-research/docs/persist-coverage-audit-2026-04-14.md` with the FULL content of the report you just sent me, verbatim. Add a 2-line header above Section 1 naming:
   - Target: v0.3 (`docs/persist-coverage-design-v0.3.md`)
   - Audit date, author attribution `(*FR:Volta*)`
   - One-line summary: "Readiness assessment — 4 scripts, mitigation choice, protocol-a draft disposition, 4 structural-discipline flags."
2. **Update** your scratchpad (`memory/volta.md`) with a CHECKPOINT one-liner:
   - `[CHECKPOINT 2026-04-14] Persist-coverage audit delivered. Full report: docs/persist-coverage-audit-2026-04-14.md. Mitigation: option (c) target-dir refusal + git check-ignore opt-in, shared helper. Ship-blockers: Flag 1 ($TEAM_DIR in skill patches) + Flag 3 (marker file before mitigation order). Draft persistence work on hold pending ship session.`
   - And a `[LEARNED]` one-liner capturing the "(c) over (a) over (b)" reasoning if you want it in your own voice — that decision rationale is worth preserving in your scratchpad, not just mine.
3. Do NOT touch any scripts. Do NOT touch `protocol-a-submission-draft.md`. Do NOT touch v0.3 design doc. Report-save + scratchpad only.
4. When done, SendMessage me a one-line confirmation: "saved [path], scratchpad updated." Team-lead commits after.

Stay in scope — report-save is a persistence operation on your own deliverable, not a design step. No rework, no new content beyond the 2-line header.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: Ack audit, save report, scratchpad checkpoint
